literature-report-plan.name: R4-regulatory-postmarket

metadata.title: Regulatory Compliance and Post-Market Surveillance for MLOps-Driven Medical AI

literature-report-plan.invocation-prompt:
create literature report on "Regulatory Compliance and Post-Market Surveillance for MLOps-Driven Medical AI"
Focus: QMSR (Feb 2026) + PCCP + IEC 62304 + TRIPOD+AI mapped to MLOps pipelines. OpenLineage as IEC 62304 Clause 8 audit trail. SBOM (CycloneDX) as FDA requirement. Post-market surveillance via drift detection + automated retraining triggers. How our factorial design IS a PCCP template (K252366 as blueprint). The locked→adaptive model lifecycle. CI/CD as production controls under QMSR. MedMLOps four pillars alignment.
Context: This repo already has OpenLineage wired into 5 flows, CycloneDX SBOM generation, and drift detection via Evidently. The fda-insights-second-pass.md covers the landscape but not the implementation mapping. This report should bridge the gap between regulatory requirements and our actual codebase.
EXCLUDED topics: - Agentic AI governance (covered in report 5 §5) - Post-training method selection (report R1) - Ensemble evaluation methodology (report R2) - Multi-site data architecture (report R3, except privacy aspects)
Repo context: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops KG domains to update: operations, infrastructure, testing Manuscript section: Discussion: Regulatory-Native MLOps (future work)

seed-papers.paper: Collins et al. (2024). "TRIPOD+AI: An updated reporting guideline for clinical prediction model studies." BMJ 385, e078378. https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj-2023-078378
Gallifant et al. (2025). "TRIPOD-LLM: Reporting guideline for studies using LLMs." Nature Medicine. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41591-024-03425-5
FDA (2021). "Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML)-Based Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) Action Plan." https://www.fda.gov/media/145022/download
de Almeida et al. (2025). "Medical machine learning operations." European Radiology. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00330-025-11654-6
Vokinger et al. (2021). "Mitigating bias in machine learning for medicine." Communications Medicine 1, 25. https://doi.org/10.1038/s43856-021-00028-w
Muehlematter et al. (2021). "Approval of AI-based medical devices in the USA and Europe." Lancet Digital Health 3(3), e195-e203. https://doi.org/10.1016/S2589-7500(20)30292-2
Pianykh et al. (2020). "Continuous Learning AI in Radiology." Radiology 297(1), 6-14. https://doi.org/10.1148/radiol.2020200038
Feng et al. (2022). "Clinical AI Quality Improvement." Nature Medicine 28, 1423-1428. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41591-022-01895-z

search-domains.domain: QMSR 2026 medical device software quality management
PCCP predetermined change control plan FDA AI ML 2025 2026
post-market surveillance AI drift detection retraining 2024 2025
TRIPOD AI reporting guideline validation clinical prediction 2025
